61096553.title: AWS S3 Bucket is not Blocking Public Access
61096553.plain_text:
scroll to the bottom for programmatic remediation of AWS S3 bucket public access
!! Important !!
If S3 bucket is serving web content ( Static Web Hosting is enabled ) follow the
Cloud Tagging Notes
optional non-security tags for remediation.
Overview
Issue
Resolved
Criteria
Existing Resource: Remediation Method
CloudFormation Example
Add PublicAccessBlockConfiguration: under Properties
Existing Resource: Remediation Impacts
Useful Links
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Programmatic remediation of AWS S3 bucket public access not blocked
You should have some familiarity with AWS Lambda, GitHub and Cloud Formation.
Cloudformation template: s3_block_public_access.yaml
It creates Lambda function with an associated schedule to run everyday. The function will check each S3 bucket. If not already blocking public access and is not for web hosting, public access blocking will be enabled unless the bucket is tagged with {"BucketType": "StaticWeb"} or {aws:cloudformation:stack-name}. Buckets with web hosting configuration will be skipped because it is not compatible with public access blocking.
Github Repo
https://github.com/pgetech/aws-s3-security-remediation
Download and save the s3_block_public_access.yaml file locally to your computer
Log into targeted AWS account and navigate to AWS cloud-formation service enter the required information.
AWS cloud formation will deploy the associated resources.  If the IAM role already exists then the account has already deployed this script and is running.
IAM
s3-block-public-access-role
Json
permissions
To verify the script has deployed check AWS lambda function called s3-block-public-access

157484265.title: AWS Access logging not enabled on S3 buckets
157484265.plain_text:
Intro:
You should have some familiarity with AWS Lambda, GitHub and Cloud Formation.
For partner accounts Scroll to the bottom regarding the repo and associated permissions to deploy the auto remediation lambda function into your accounts
To check S3 server access logging is enabled go to the suspect S3 bucket > properties and scroll down to server access logging.
Verify the S3 account has the target logging bucket. Target logging bucket follows the following naming convention
ccoe-s3-accesslogs-spoke-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::AccountId}
2.Check the S3 bucket > properties resource Tags.
If Bucket Tag key contains "cloud formation" then you must update and deploy the template to enable S3 access logging to future proof any template change and undoing any manual alteration to enable server access logging.
CF( cloud formation )
Template
implementation of enabling access logging
To programmatically enable S3 access logging. You must update the associated GitHub repository. From the troubled bucket cloud formation stack name tag. You will be able to trace the deployment method and associated GitHub Repo.
Once you have identified the GitHub Repository input the following configuration in the S3 bucket resource under properties.
Official doc
***Note make sure to substitute
pBucketName to the S3 bucket resource name***
---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
LoggingConfiguration:
DestinationBucketName:
!Sub ccoe-s3-accesslogs-spoke-${AWS::Region}-${AWS::AccountId}
LogFilePrefix: !Sub ${pBucketName}/
----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
b.
If Bucket Tag key does not contain a "cloud formation". Then you can enable S3 access logging via a lambda function.
For auto remediation
Enter into shared services Prod account and search for lambda function  "
s3-access-logging"
Move the test tab and run the Lambda function remember to input the account that the S3 is in for remediation
example input
{
"AccountId": "686137062481"
}
3. Run the lambda function and S3 access logging should be enabled for all buckets within the targeted account.
PARTNERS
Please find the
GitHub Repository Cloud Formation Template
for access logs.
****
note
the function without proper permissions will generate an error due to the fact that the input parameter takes an account number that could be different in which the function is deployed. Thus you must add an sts assume role to the lambda function. ****
Once deployed to your selected account. open the lambda function and browse to selected execution role.
Browse to create an inline policy amd select JASON TAb
Enter the following configuration and you can begin executing the code
{
"Version": "2012-10-17",
"Statement": [
{
"Action": [
"lambda:InvokeFunction",
"s3:ListAllMyBuckets",
"s3:GetBucketWebsite",
"s3:GetBucketTagging",
"s3:PutBucketLogging",
"s3:GetBucketLocation"
],
"Resource": "*",
"Effect": "Allow"
},
{
"Action": [
"logs:CreateLogGroup",
"logs:CreateLogStream",
"logs:PutLogEvents"
],
"Resource": "arn:aws:logs:*:*:*",
"Effect": "Allow"
},
{
"Action": "sts:AssumeRole",
"Resource": "arn:aws:iam::*:role/AWSCloudFormationStackSetExecutionRole",
"Effect": "Allow"
}
]
}
once the Lambda Permissions have been updated you can move back to the lambda function
Move the test tab and run the Lambda function remember to input the account that the S3 is in for remediation
example input
{
"AccountId": "686137062481"
}

79562150.title: AWS S3 Missing Required Tags
79562150.plain_text:
Cloud COE has developed an automated programmatic remediation of 'S3 buckets missing required tags' through the use of a Lambda function that is scheduled to run daily.
This Lambda function will check all S3 buckets in the account which were not created by ClouFormation or Terraform.
Note:
Buckets created by CloudFormation or Terraform will be skipped by this Lambda function, and should be remediated through changes in the stack tags. This Function will not over write existing mandatory tags and will only add the mandatory tags that are missing.
Cloud Tagging Standard
Mandatory tags:
AppID
Environment
DataClassification
CRIS
Notify
Owner
Compliance
Git Repo CF template
https://github.com/pgetech/aws-s3-security-remediation/blob/master/s3_add_missing_tags.yaml
Steps:
1) Download or store the CF template locally
- If using git you can clone the file/repo using the following command
git clone
https://github.com/pgetech/aws-s3-security-remediation.git
example shown below
2) Deploy template
Log in the AWS account with sufficient permissions (admin or above to be able to deploy resources) and navigate to AWS cloud formation service in the search bar
In cloud formation head to stacks and create stack new resources
Upload CF stack and complete Required tags that will be added to non compliant resources.
Navigate to Lambda function and configure the required test event.
The lambda function has a time out of 3 minutes
.

76586242.title: AWS S3 bucket is not using PGE managed KMS key
76586242.plain_text:
Remediation
Per PG&E CS-TECH-020 Standard, customer managed keys (CMKs) must be used with 'Confidential' or 'Restricted' data. AWS managed keys (KMS) are approved for general encryption at rest requirements.
In Cloud COE accounts, we use this
Cloudformation template
to create a CMK with alias name servicekey/s3 and store its ARN in parameter store /servicekey/s3.  You can use the Cloudformation template as a reference to create CMK in partner's account, but you have to modify the key policy to include your IAM roles.
If you have other AWS resources (e.g. IAM role, Lambda, EC2 etc) access the CMK encrypted buckets, you have to add the following permissions in their resource role policy:
for S3 read access: kms:Decrypt
for S3 write access: kms:Encrypt, kms:GenerateDataKey
Note for S3 buckets accessed by Storage Gateway
If your S3 bucket is configured for Storage Gateway file share access, please update CMK key policy to allow use of the key by principal arn:aws:iam::972333128287:root
Sid: Allow use of the key by Storage Gateway account
Effect: Allow
Principal:
AWS:
- !Sub arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/SuperAdmin
- !Sub arn:aws:iam::${AWS::AccountId}:role/CloudAdmin
- "arn:aws:iam::972333128287:root"
Action:
- 'kms:Encrypt'
- 'kms:Decrypt'
- 'kms:ReEncrypt*'
- 'kms:GenerateDataKey*'
- 'kms:DescribeKey'
Resource: '*'
Using S3 console
In the bucket's Properties tab, edit the Default Encryption.
Using Cloudformation
Parameters:
pCmkArn:
Description: Parameter store of the S3 CMK ARN for bucket encryption
Type: 'AWS::SSM::Parameter::Value<String>'
Default: '/servicekey/landingzone'
Resources:
rS3bucket:
Type: AWS::S3::Bucket
Properties:
BucketEncryption:
ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration:
- ServerSideEncryptionByDefault:
SSEAlgorithm: "aws:kms"
KMSMasterKeyID: !Ref pCmkArn
BucketKeyEnabled: true
Using SDK boto3 API - Python
def encrypt_bucket_with_cmk( bucket_name, cmk_arn ):
try:
response = s3_client.put_bucket_encryption(
Bucket=bucket_name,
ServerSideEncryptionConfiguration={
'Rules': [
{
'ApplyServerSideEncryptionByDefault': {
'SSEAlgorithm': 'aws:kms',
'KMSMasterKeyID': cmk_arn
},
'BucketKeyEnabled': True
},
]
}
)

118918423.title: AWS S3 bucket is not logging to central logging S3 bucket
118918423.plain_text:
Given
a Brinqa ticket of type, Prisma Cloud, with account_id and resource_name of an S3 Bucket,
When
that S3 bucket access log is configured with centralized logging bucket and label
Then
CSCOE automation will close that Brinqa ticket.
Step-by-step guide
Add the steps involved:
Switch into the given account.
Open the S3 Console and find the bucket with the given resource_name.
Navigate to the Bucket/Properties Tab
Scroll down to
Server access logging
press Edit.
Enable Server access logging
Populate Target bucket with the s3 address of the bucket, ccoe-s3-accesslogs-spoke-region-account#/ .
Specify a subfolder, aka label, by appending this bucket's name to the Target bucket field.
Push Save Changes.
Related articles
Related articles appear here based on the labels you select. Click to edit the macro and add or change labels.

AWS S3 bucket is not using PGE managed KMS key

61096551.title: AWS S3 bucket without SecureTransport policy
61096551.plain_text:
Overview
When you receive a Brinqa ticket with the summary "
AWS S3 bucket without SecureTransport policy". This ticket indicates that your bucket policy does not have a
Deny
for traffic that is not using
SecureTransport
. To remediate this ticket please ensure that you update your bucket policy. Examples of how to remediate this via JSON (directly from the S3 console) or from CF template can be found below.
How To Remediate the Bucket Policy JSON
Ensure that you have the below JSON block added to your Bucket Policy to
Deny
access to your buckets when
SecureTransport
is not being used. This policy can be updated directly from the S3 web console or CLI but we suggest that you use infrastructure as code (TF, CFT) as a best practice. More information on how to use the S3 console can be found here
https://docs.aws.amazon.com/AmazonS3/latest/userguide/example-policies-s3.html
Sample Bucket Policy to remediate your Brinqa ticket
{
"Version"
:
"2012-10-17"
,
"Statement"
: [
{
"Effect"
:
"Deny"
,
"Principal"
:
"*"
,
"Action"
:
"s3:*"
,
"Resource"
: [
"arn:aws:s3:::examplebucket/*"
,
"arn:aws:s3:::examplebucket"
],
"Condition"
: {
"Bool"
: {
"aws:SecureTransport"
:
"false"
}
}
}
]
}
How to Remediate the Bucket Policy Via CloudFormation Template
The example YAML CF template block shows how to create and attach a bucket policy which has a
Deny
for traffic that is not using
SecureTranport
for a bucket with the resource name rExampleBucket.
rExampleBucketPolicy:
Type:
'AWS::S3::BucketPolicy'
Properties:
Bucket: !Ref rExampleBucket
PolicyDocument:
Version:
'2012-10-17'
Statement:
- Sid: AWSSecureTransport
Effect: Deny
Principal:
'*'
Action:
's3:*'
Resource:
- !Sub
'arn:aws:s3:::${rExampleBucket}'
- !Sub
'arn:aws:s3:::${rExampleBucket}/*'
Condition:
Bool:
aws:SecureTransport:
false
Resolved
Criteria

118918659.plain_text:
Given
a Brinqa ticket of type, Prisma Cloud, with account_id and age of secret manager key > 90 (DateTime.ageInDays(lastChangedDate) > 90)

192349205.title: Configure PingFederate for Multi Factor Authentication (MFA)
192349205.plain_text:
This details below involve instructions on how to set up either a SAML or OAuth application to use MFA.
SAML MFA Setup
Enable MFA for a SAML application
Add SAML Connection to the MFA SAML App Selector (may be called just MFAApp in some environments)
Remove SAML Connection from the associated Selector - most likely Kerberos & Form - PGE
Replicate changes
OAuth MFA Setup
Enable MFA for an OAuth/OIDC application
Add OAuth client to the MFA OAuth App Selector (aka MFAOAuth)
Remove OAuth Client from the associated Selector - most likely Kerberos & Form - PGE OAuth
Replicate changes

334373272.title: PG&E Entra ID Client Credential OAuth Flow (System to System Authentication)
334373272.plain_text:
PG&E
Entra ID
Client Credential OAuth Flow
This document
provides
steps
to perform
certificate-based
client authentication
for
OAuth flow.
Certificate based authentication is
a
secure
authentication than the traditional
password/
secret-based
authentication.
Since
Entra ID (formerly known as Azure AD)
will be PG&E’s new Identity provider aka
OAuth authorization server, this document will focus on
integration with
Entra ID
.
Client authentication is
important in the OAuth flow
. It
becomes
more
important in Client Credentials grant flow
because of
it being the only authentication
requirement
to obtain bearer token.
Steps to
obtain Bearer token in Client credentials OAuth flow
-
1.Generate a
certificate:
OAuth
clients
will need to
generate
a certificate and share the
public
cert with
Azure
SSO team.
SSO
team will upload the public cert in the
Entra ID
for the OAuth connection. OAuth client in this case
is
the application trying to obtain bearer token from
Entra ID
.
Certificate for non-
prod
connection can be self-signed. For prod certificate should be CA
signed (
can be internal PG&E CA
signed
)
. CN
and DNS
value of the
certificate
should be in th
e
format -
{
A
pp
ID
}
-{
Application
Name
}
.
EntraID.AuthN.pge.com
Tip:
U
se tools like OpenSSL to generate the certificate.
openssl req -x509 -sha256 -nodes -days
365
-newkey rsa:
4096
-keyout
private
.key -out mycert.crt
2.O
btain the x
5t header parameter
for the JWT
:
X5t is Base64url-encoded SHA-1 thumbprint of the X.509 certificate's DER
encoding.
Tip: OpenSSL has commands to generate
x5t. one such command is
- echo $(
openssl
x509 -in mycert.
crt
-fingerprint -
noout
) | sed 's/SHA1 Fingerprint=//g' | sed 's/://g' |
xxd
-r -
ps
| base64
3.Construct JWT
:
JWT
has 3 parts
–
a.
Header
: It
contains
the information
about the
key and the algorithm used to generate the signature.
Sample header-
{
"
alg
": "RS256",
"
typ
": "JWT",
"x5t": "Py3f8vKDUjR3bqfUhzMIyOb8XuM="
}
Parameter
Remark
alg
Should be RS256
typ
Should be JWT
X5t
Explained in step#
2 ,
Base64url-encoded SHA-1 thumbprint of the X.509 certificate's DER encoding.
b.
Pay load
:
C
ontains
set of claims
.
Sample payload
-
{
"
aud
": "
https://login.microsoftonline.com/e84bb675-81b1-4f49-985b-3e2d77c6c94d/oauth2/v2.0/token
",
"exp": 1689303330,
"
iss
": "c1127ab5-330a-46ed-b6a9-039795748df9",
"
jti
": "22b3bb26-e046-42df-9c96-65dbd72c1c81",
"
nbf
": 1484592741,
"sub": "c1127ab5-330a-46ed-b6a9-039795748df9"
,
"
iat
": 1484592741,
}
Claim
Sample value
Description
aud
https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenantId}/oauth2/v2.0/token
The "
aud
" (audience) claim
identifies
the recipients that the
JWT is intended for (Azure AD)
exp
The "exp" (
expiration
time) claim
identifies
the
expiration
time on or after which the JWT MUST NOT be accepted for processing. See
RFC 7519, Section 4.1.4
. This allows the assertion to be used until then, so keep it short - 5-10 minutes after
nbf
at most. Azure AD does
not place
restrictions on the
exp
time currently
.
Iss
ClientID
The "
iss
" (issuer) claim
identifies
the principal that issued the JWT, in this case your
client
application. Use the GUID application ID.
jti
The "
jti
" (JWT ID) claim provides a unique identifier for the JWT. The identifier value MUST be assigned in a manner that ensures that there is a negligible probability that the same value will be accidentally assigned to a different data object; if the application uses multiple issuers, collisions MUST be prevented among values produced by different issuers as well. The "
jti
" value is a case-sensitive
string.
RFC 7519, Section 4.1.7
nbf
The "
nbf
" (not before) claim
identifies
the time before which the JWT MUST NOT be accepted for processing.
RFC 7519, Section 4.1.5
. Using the current time is
appropriate
.
sub
ClientID
The "sub" (subject) claim
identifies
the subject of the JWT, in this case also your application. Use the same value as
iss
.
iat
The "
iat
" (issued at) claim
identifies
the time at which the JWT was issued. This claim can be used to
determine
the age of the JWT.
RFC 7519, Section 4.1.5
.
c.
Signature
:
S
ignature allows a JWT to be validated against modifications and
it
confirms the authenticity of the
JWT
.
4. Call Entra ID to obtain bearer token:
Make a
POST
call to
Entra ID
token end point -
https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant ID}/oauth2/v2.0/token
with following OAuth parameters in the body
.
client_id
–
T
his is the client identifier.
The value
for this parameter will be provided by
Azure
SSO team.
grant_type
=
client_credentials
scope
-
This defines the access granted to the bearer token.
The value
for this will be provided
by
Azure
SSO team.
client_assertion_type
=
urn:ietf
:params:oauth:client-assertion-type:jwt-bearer
client_assertion
–
B
ase 64 encoded JWT
constructed in step#3 above.
If OAuth parameters in the request are correct and authentication is successful
,
Entra ID
will provide
access
token in response.
Sample response:
{"token_type":"Bearer","expires_in":3599,"ext_expires_in":3599,"access_token":"
yJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsIng1dCI6IlhSdmtvOFA3QTNVYVdTblU3Yk05blQwTWpoQSIsImtpZCI6IlhSdmtvOFA3QTNVYVdTblU3Yk05blQwTWpoQSJ9..sJyB8_9YimSP9YArshSZf9yaGsUATBSm7KAuq1cJ8fPcIfzdLHGfvkDXlry6echT1PganBYQhy3kgN24ysGtKwqbbhroytbcbA_5Zoki3o-7CrNPmMoJR2AjVPLDgkid76bvYgPEg_hCb9xLWCVqOXubA7gwVI-vCHtPBpT8nT6r3CnyEtMy0yea2MorCryyZUyatOe4ptYq_28rnVx7CKxbhGmvC5TG3AxzMdI3I1FdRie_AIvp
2Mm
Gf7autpWvQknj47PdgS4mw3V47qovKFnOLpgstQ-VI3bCYyhe5qokpNWffBmoA17q4mR5e60sr9JzAQxyP_nT39RlwOCV7
"}
Decoded token value -
{
"
aud
": "
api
://
69019a41-8eab-4961-a8b7-0fed123eebc0.lab.pge.com
",
"
iss
": "
https://sts.windows.net/e84bb675-81b1-4f49-985b-3e2d77c6c94d/
",
"
iat
": 1710263293,
"
nbf
": 1710263293,
"exp": 1710267193,
"
aio
": "E2NgYBBTsP6z+/xWf95XXdMuv7/
mBAA
=",
"
appid
": "69019a41-8eab-4961-a8b7-0fed123eebc0",
"
appidacr
": "1",
"
idp
": "
https://sts.windows.net/e84bb675-81b1-4f49-985b-3e2d77c6c94d/
",
"
oid
": "ed131581-28cb-4112-9833-a138e0fefda5",
"rh": "
0.AScAdbZL
6LGBSU-YWz4td8bJTUGaAWmrjmFJqLcP7RI-68AnAAA.",
"roles": [
"811api",
"
ClientApp
=
<
ApplicationName
>
"
],
"sub": "ed131581-28cb-4112-9833-a138e0fefda5",
"
tid
": "e84bb675-81b1-4f49-985b-3e2d77c6c94d",
"
uti
": "bLcCvgDjw0OxC1X-h310AA",
"
ver
": "1.0"
}
Please note one of the role values("
ClientApp
=
<
ApplicationName
>
") in the token payload
contains
Client Application name. Resource server can
also check
this value to
determine
the client application name
and
make an authorization decision to its API resources.
Microsoft documentation on this topic:
OAuth 2.0 client credentials flow on the Microsoft identity platform - Microsoft Entra | Microsoft Learn
-
look for
section “
Second case: Access token request with a
certificate.
”

196347123.title: Intake a new SAML or OAuth Application
196347123.plain_text:
These notes are for the Ping or Azure SSO Admin Team, not for the Application Teams,,,
How to intake a new SAML or Auth Application (the process for PingAccess is slightly different).
Most application teams should first work with the MEA team to get their AD groups created.  They likely have filled out the Rapid Onboarding form, so you may be able to get some information from
https://rapidonboarding.cloud.pge.com
.
Ask the application team to fill out the SSO Intake questionnaire at:
SSO Questionnaire.xlsx
Ask the application team to create a Work Order using:
CyberSecurity Services (pge.com)
-  Service = Authentication
Add an entry to the CommSheet spreadsheet on the appropriate tab (New Integration) at:
CommSheet.xlsx
.
Confirm that AD groups have been created (for PROD Tenant) and tenant-only groups have been added to AAD Dev for lower environments, where required.
For Ping deployments, create a Standard CRQ for lower environments using the PingFederate -  Lower Environment Changes template.  For PROD deployment, create a Standard CRQ using the New PingFederate SSO Integration template.  Add Anton or Ping Operations group for Approvals before the Scheduled stage.  Call ENOC to start and end CRQs.
For Azure deployments, no CRQ is required for changes to the PGEDev Tenant.  For PROD changes, create a Standard CRQ using the Azure AD SSO - Configure new application template.  Add Anton or Ping Operations group for Approvals before the Scheduled stage.  Call ENOC to start and end CRQ.

195036793.title: Move an application - Post move follow up steps
195036793.plain_text:
After migrating an application to a new location, here are two recommended steps to ensure the application is found during reboots and other automated procedures
Create a Link from the old location to the new
Create a link to ensure the application can be found in the old location.
If the application used to live at /opt/appname and you moved it to /app/appname, run this command after moving /opt/appname:
ln -s /app/appname /opt/appname
The first directory listed in the command is the new location and the second directory is the old location.
Create a WO for the Linux Ops team to update INIT scripts
Locate the associated INIT scripts in /etc/init.d.
Copy any impacted scripts to a temporary location
Edit the temporary location scripts and update the PATH or location of the binaries
Create a Work Order (WO) for the UNIX/Linux team at:
https://iis10t2prd.cloud.pge.com/myitservices/forms/intake/453
Follow up with an email to "IT I&O IO SNO Unix Linux Ops NEW" Distribution List (DL) if not completed prior to the next patch or planned reboot of the server(s) in question.

195037155.plain_text:
If you need to change where PingFederate stores the Session Store (used for Persistent Grants), you need to follow these steps.
Add new Data Store to PingFederate using Admin UI
Copy the System ID for the new Data Store from the PF Data Store page
Backup and copy the AccessGrantManagerLDAPOracleImpl.xml file
As pingfed user:
Archive
/opt/pingfederate/admin/current/pingfederate/server/default/data/config-store/org.sourceid.oauth20.token.AccessGrantManagerLDAPOracleImpl.xml
vi
/opt/pingfederate/admin/current/pingfederate/server/default/data/config-store/org.sourceid.oauth20.token.AccessGrantManagerLDAPOracleImpl.xml
Change these two entries:
<c:item name="PingFederateDSJNDIName">LDAP-E956C06CFB589519E9E89E2958778EE55A8B0491</c:item>
^ Enter the new Data Store System ID
<c:item name="SearchBase">ou=AccessGrantsDev,dc=utility,dc=pge,dc=com</c:item>
^ LDAP Search Base
Restart PingFederate Admin instance
Replicate using Cluster Management in Admin UI
Restart PingFederate Engines

189563590.title: Certificates and PKI
189563590.plain_text:
As of March 2023, we are going to assist with approving SSL certificate requests outside of business hours.
Instructions
To administer the PKI Certificates, you will need access to KeyFactor Portal
https://pgecerts.cloud.pge.com/Keyfactorportal/CertificateRequests
Job aid:
PKI Certificates - iPRiME Wiki - PGE Wiki
Login to the KeyFactor Portal
Open an Incognito session in Chrome
Login with your Admin Credentials
Click on Workflow tab the drop-down menu and select Certificate Requests
Select the certificate and click details to review the metadata
Make sure the spelling is accurate, PG&E needs to be spelled out like (Pacific Gas and Electric Company)- Needs to be spelled out as in parenthesis
-Always should be San Francisco or Oakland location
-State should be California
-Country should be US
-Organization unit (OU) should list the environment (Development, Production, Test, or QA) make sure they spell each thing out as listed in parenthesis
Common name needs to be listed within three fields: Common Name, distinguished name field, and in the subject alternative names.
Subject Alt Name: one person listed, make sure they are not a contractor, look in JAT, (JAT will show Account expire date for a contractor).
-Common Name: FQDN of the server
The order could be different but as long as all of the values are included.
After you review approve or deny the request. Supply a comment if appropriate. The system will notify the user of the approval or rejection.

195035471.title: Patching Validation
195035471.plain_text:
How to validate Ping and ODSEE/OUD applications after patching.
PingFederate
Login to PingFederate Admin console and confirm that all patched servers are showing up under System → Cluster Management.  In PROD, there should be a total of 10 objects listed, if all servers are up and running.
Here is an example of all PingFederate PROD servers up and running:
Also, use Splunk or go to /logs/pf_engine on all patched PF Engines and check that all instances are running and there are no unusual errors.
Here are two errors to check for.  If you see them, see if one PingFederate engine IP address shows up more often than the others in the first error, and restart PingFederate on that server.
index=ping "Unable to retrieve artifact from"
index=ping "Unexpected problem looking up authorization code"
PingFederate PROD servers
pritappgenlc023
pritappgenlc024
pritappgenlc025
pritappgenlc026
pritappgenlc027
pritappgenlc028
pritappgenlc029
pritappgenlc030
OUD Session Store
PingFederate relies on OUD for a Sesson Store.  At this time (April 2023), OUD runs on the PingFederate Admin instances (as part of Application Health Initiative, OUD will be moved to its own servers).  Validate that OUD is running with:
ps -ef | grep oud
The above should return a single process that looks like this:
pingfed  44183     1  0 Mar31 ?        00:35:15 /usr/lib/jvm/jdk1.8.0_112/jre/bin/java -Xms2056m -Xmx2056m -d64 -XX:+UseCompressedOops -server -Xmn512m -XX:MaxTenuringThreshold=1 -XX:+UseConcMarkSweepGC -XX:CMSInitiatingOccupancyFraction=55 -Dorg.opends.server.scriptName=start-ds org.opends.server.core.DirectoryServer --configClass org.opends.server.extensions.ConfigFileHandler --configFile /app/oud/Middleware/PINGPRD/OUD/config/config.ldif
Also check the OUD logs at /app/oud/Middleware/PINGPRD/OUD/logs/errors for any unusual errors.
If OUD didn't start or needs to be restarted, it can be stopped and started with these commands (run as pingfed user):
/app/oud/Middleware/PINGPRD/OUD/bin/stop-ds
/app/oud/Middleware/PINGPRD/OUD/bin/start-ds
OUD PROD Servers
pritappgenlc023 (primary)
pritappgenlc027 (secondary)
PingAccess
Login to PingAccess Admin console and confirm that all patched servers are showing up under System → Clustering.  In PROD, there should be a total of 6 Engines listed, if all servers are up and running.
Here is an example of all PingAccess PROD servers up and running:
Also, use Splunk or go to /logs/pa_engine on all patched PA Engines and check that all instances are running and there are no unusual errors.
PingAccess PROD servers
PRITAPPGENLC043 (Primary Admin)
PRITAPPGENLC044
PRITAPPGENLC046
PRITAPPGENLC047
PRITAPPGENLC048 (Secondary Admin)
PRITAPPGENLC051
PRITAPPGENLC052
PRITAPPGENLC053
ODSEE
ODSEE is used to store external user authentication data.  It must be up and running for external-facing applications using PingFederate to authenticate.
Validate that ODSEE is running with:
ps -ef | grep slapd
The above should return a single process that looks like this:
slapd     2640     1  0 Feb10 ?        03:51:49 /app/dsee7/lib/64/ns-slapd -D /app/dsee7/slapd-user -i /app/dsee7/slapd-user/logs/pid
Also check for unusual errors in /logs/slapd/user/errors.  You can also use Apache Directory Studio to login to auth.comp.pge.com and run an LDAP query.
ODSEE PROD Servers
itauthprd01 (Primary)
itauthprd02
itauthprd11
itauthprd12

It supports integration with Microsoft Entra ID, Azure Key Vault, and virtual networks to enhance security and governance.

Azure Redis integrates with Azure Virtual Network, Microsoft Entra ID, and diagnostic tools to provide secure, scalable, and resilient caching solutions.

342299090.plain_text:

In Progress
Azure App Service is a platform that lets you run web applications, mobile back ends, and RESTful APIs without worrying about managing the underlying infrastructure. Think of it as a powerful web hosting service that takes care of all the heavy lifting for you, so can focus on creating great applications.
App Service supports a variety of web stacks: .NET, Java (in Java SE, Tomcat, and JBoss flavors), Node.js, Python, and PHP, and can run them on both Windows and Linux. Or, if your app is containerized, you can just deploy it as a custom container.

342302934.plain_text:
Control Definition
In Progress
Control Development
In Progress
Azure Key Vault is a cloud service for securely storing and accessing secrets. A secret is anything that you want to tightly control access to, such as API keys, passwords, certificates, or cryptographic keys. Key Vault service supports two types of containers: vaults and managed hardware security module (HSM) pools. Vaults support storing software and HSM-backed keys, secrets, and certificates. Managed HSM pools only support HSM-backed keys.

342299132.title: Azure SQL Database
342299132.plain_text:
Azure SQL Database is always running on the latest stable version of the SQL Server database engine and patched OS with
99.99% availability
. PaaS capabilities built into Azure SQL Database enable you to focus on the domain-specific database administration and optimization activities that are critical for your business. With Azure SQL Database, you can create a highly available and high-performance data storage layer for the applications and solutions in Azure.
SQL Databse Policies:
Control Type
Category
Severity
Controls
Description
Compliance Mapping
Wiz Policy Enforced
Azure Policy Enforced
Access Control and Identity Mgmt
Security
HIGH
SQL Database vulnerability assessment should send email notifications to admins and subscription owners
This rule checks whether the SQL Database Vulnerability Assessment (VA) periodic recurring scans are being sent to admins and subscription owners. This rule fails if `DatabaseSecurityAlertPolicy.properties.state` is `enabled`, indicating that VA periodic scans are on, and `DatabaseSecurityAlertPolicy.properties.emailAccountAdmins` is `false`. SQL Vulnerability Assessment is a feature of Microsoft Azure Defender for SQL. It is an easy-to-configure service that can discover, track, and help you remediate potential database vulnerabilities. It is recommended to use it to proactively improve your database security and enable the `Also send email notifications to admins and subscription owners` setting.
Logging and Monitoring
Security
HIGH
SQL Database Threat Detection log retention should be set to 365 or greater
This rule checks whether the SQL database is enabled and has a retention period of 365 days or more for threat detection events. Azure SQL Database Threat Detection is a security intelligence feature that identifies potential threats to the database. This feature detects potential vulnerabilities, SQL injection attacks, and anomalous database access patterns. The retention period defines the number of days to retain threat detection events.
Encryption and Key Management
Security
HIGH
SQL Database Ledger should be enabled
This rule checks whether Ledger is enabled on Azure SQL databases. Enabling the Ledger on Azure SQL databases provides an additional layer of security by providing a tamper-evident ledger for tracking and auditing changes over time. It is recommended to enable this feature to enhance the immutability and auditability of the data within the database.
Encryption and Key Management
Security
HIGH
SQL Database transparent data encryption should be enabled
This rule checks whether transparent data encryption (TDE) is enabled on the SQL Database. This rule fails if the SQL Database is not a master database and if `TransparentDataEncryption.properties.state` is set to `Disabled`. Azure SQL Database transparent data encryption helps protect against malicious activity by performing real-time encryption and decryption of the database, associated backups, and transaction log files at rest without requiring changes to the application.
System Management Configuration
Security
HIGH
SQL Database should be zone redundant
This rule checks whether the Azure SQL Database is configured to be zone redundant. Zone redundancy enables high availability and protects your Azure SQL Database from datacenter-level failures by automatically replicating your data across multiple availability zones.
Logging and Monitoring
Security
HIGH
SQL Database should have Threat Detection enabled
This rule checks whether the SQL database has Threat Detection enabled. Azure SQL Database Threat Detection is a security intelligence feature that identifies potential threats to the database. This feature detects potential vulnerabilities, SQL injection attacks, and anomalous database access patterns.
Infrastructure Security
Security
HIGH
SQL Database should not use 'Basic' SKU
This rule checks if the SQL Database is using the Basic or Consumption SKU pricing tier. The Basic SKU is a legacy SKU and has feature limitations such as disabled Service Level Agreements (SLAs). It is recommended to upgrade the SKU tier to maintain a higher level of availability and performance.
Access Control and Identity Mgmt
Security
HIGH
SQL Database should be configured to send Threat Detection email alerts
This rule checks whether the threat detection policy of the SQL database is configured to send email alerts. This rule fails if 'emailAddresses' is empty. Azure SQL Database Threat Detection is a security intelligence feature that identifies potential threats to the database. Ensure the SQL database sends threat detection alerts to the relevant email addresses.

345421553.title: Azure Storage Accounts
345421553.plain_text:
Azure Storage Accounts provide scalable, durable, and secure cloud storage for a variety of data objects including blobs, files, queues, tables, and disks. They support multiple redundancy options and integrate with Azure services for secure access, encryption, and monitoring. This service is critical for storing application data, logs, backups, and more.

283740473.title: App Proxy Connector Deployment
283740473.plain_text:
Overview
A Windows EC2 will be spun up with App Proxy Connector software in it using Terraform Cloud Pipeline which also includes the required script that aids in registering the connector to an Entra tenant.
TFC Pipeline:
Dev
Prod
GitHub Repo:
Dev
Prod
Before running the Terraform Cloud Pipeline make sure the following are set properly for a successful terraform run
Terraform variables
account_num
=
AWS
Account number where you want to create EC2 instance
aws_region
=
Closest AWS Region
instance_proflie_name
= Existing instance profile for EC2 or use aws_service and policy_arns to create a role
aws_role
=
"CloudAdmin"
kms_role
=
"CloudAdmin"
aws_service
=
Optional. List of AWS services allowed to assume the custom role (if opted for a custom role instead of default instance_profile)
policy_arns
=
Optional. List of policy ARNs incase a custom instance_profile for EC2 has to be created. A default role is available
windows-ec2-golden-role
which can be set in TF Var
instance_profile_name
AppID
=
Identify the application this asset belongs to by its AMPS APP ID.Format = APP-####"
Environment
=
"Dev, Test, QA, Prod (only one)
DataClassification
=
"Internal"
name
=
Name of the EC2 instance that gets created.
CRIS
=
"Cyber Risk Impact Score High, Medium, Low (only one)"
Notify
=
Who to notify for system failure or maintenance. Should be a group or list of email addresses.
Owner
=
List three owners of the system, as defined by AMPS Director, Client Owner and IT Leader
Compliance
=
[
"None"]
Instances Object
hibernation
= If true EC2 goes on hibernation during off hours
ami_id
= Ami id for the EC2 instance
instance_type
= Type of the EC2 instance to be created
availability_zone
= Zone where EC2
windows_admin_user
= optional. If domain join is selected as activity, this id is used for setting up
additional_security_group_ids
= optional, if there are any additional security groups to be attached
adproxy_service_account
= optional. If register_domain is activity, this user account is used for registering App Proxy connector
domain_name
= optional. If domain join is selected as activity, name of the domain to join
tenant_id
= string
activities
= Optional. If domain join is required set domain_join , register_domain if App Proxy Connector has to be registered.
dns_ips
= optional. If domain join is selected as activity, these DNS iPs are set
Setup in AWS Account
Secrets manager
This is required for domain joining only. A secret with name cscoe-aad-win-appproxy-papm-pwd with key value <username>_papm_pwd where username is a variable in TF Vars and respective password.
Required for
registering the connector
. A secret with name adproxy-service-account-pwd with key <username-for-app-registration> where username is a variable in
TF Vars
and respective password.
Role
Terraform variable
instance_profile_name
will be set to
windows-ec2-golden-role,
which would have already been created with account setup. Make sure this role is present. You can also setup a role of your own by giving the policy arns in TF Vars
Terraform variable values in Parameter Store:
These are mostly set in every account but ensure these terraform variables have respective keys from AWS Parameter store
For ex.
vpc_id
terraform variable in
TF Vars
should carry a value of /vpc/id
subnet_id1
: like vpc_id above
golden_ami
: should be set to the custom AMI for App Proxy which holds the App Proxy connector software that can be registered.
Security Groups
additional_security_groups
: These security groups are created in every account, make sure these
security group ids (NOT security group names)
are passed in TF variable and are set in AWS account.
Verification
Once tfvars are setup in dev/prod branches, committing changes in those respective branches initiates a Terraform Cloud Pipeline (
dev
) with completed Terraform plan phase. Apply the changes by clicking the 'Apply' in TFC pipeline after verifying changes in plan phase.
Logon to Systems manager in EC2(by selecting the EC2 and clicking on 'Connect' on top right in AWS UI) that is just spun up and get a host name
Logon to App Proxy in Entra and verify the instance

App Proxy Architecture Diagram
High level view
Implementation at PGE
CSCOE creates Enterprise App Proxy application in Entra, following information is required from the application team...
Internal URL that is used to access the application
(Optional) List of users that require access to the application (Not Recommended check C.)
(Recommended) Project Manager/Owner fills the
SSO questionnaire
and attaches it to a
Front door
request, this would place a work order for Abhishek Chauhan to set up SSO. Result will be an Entitlement created in MEA , respective AD group in Entra. Examples...
Entitlement
Group in Entra
First, application will be created with <app-name>-<tenant>.<domain>.  For example, if application name is 'workviz' and domain as -pge.msappproxy.net, it would be workviz-pge.msappproxy.net where PGE is the tenant.
Once the application is created, either individual users are added to this application or MEA mapped AD group is attached for access to users.
Application team will try to access the application. incase of any issues they can reach out to CSCOE. Some of the issues that other applications have faced are given below with solutions.
After successfully completing above steps, Step 2, 3 and 4 will be repeated with custom domain.
Issues
Sorry, but we are having trouble signing you in
. User doesn't have access, reach out to CSCOE for access or SSO is configured, user needs to request for respective entitlement in MEA
403 Error
. Ensure CIDR for app proxy connector is added to WAF firewall allow list. You can see the steps
here
.
400 Invalid_redirect_uri
: When customer lands on main page after authentication, any re-routings from that page are errored. Entra has no settings related to re-routing, corresponding application team needs to check their settings.

279021283.plain_text:
for example if App Proxy Connector is running in a network which has access to application.
We create an Enterprise application in Entra that represents on-prem/cloud application, map this application with App Proxy connector and manager user assignment to this Enterprise application. Homepage URL given by Enterprise application can be used to authenticate the users and access the application.
Setting up Azure Shell
Login into portal.azure.com and sign-in to it. After successful sign-in, click on top right to open new Azure shell session.
Open the editor from Shell session where files can be managed.
You can clone the repo or simply copy this
file
into the editor.
Run 'connect-azuread' and 'connect-mggraph' commands and complete any verifications required.
Creating Enterprise Application or Service Principal
Prepare to execute the PowerShell script...
Example: .
/application-management.ps1 -application_name appToBeDeleted05 -external_domain pgedev.msappproxy.net -internal_url https://mediawiki2.nonprod.pge.com/ -connector_group_name ToBeDeletedGroup
<filename>.ps1
-application_name
- Name of the application
-external_domain
- Domain where we are creating this application for ex.
pgedev.msappproxy.net
-internal_url
- Actual application URL (should be access to App Proxy connector either public or in same network where App Proxy connector is )  ex.
https://mediawiki2.nonprod.pge.com/
-connector_group_name
- App Proxy Connector group name
Accept any installations and execute the PowerShell script.
Go to portal.azure.com. → Search for Enterprise Applications → All Applications → Search for the application you created.    Look for 'users and groups' on the left menu with in the application and click on 'Add New User'
Add User/s or Group/s who requires access to the application and click on save.
User accessing the application
User can go to
https://myapps.microsoft.com
and sign-in with his credentials (once user is added to application it may take up to 5 mins for app to be visible in myapps).
User can click on the app and continue signing-in . After successful signing-in, he will be redirected to the application

279020307.title: Get Custom Attributes - Graph API
279020307.plain_text:
Using Graph Explorer
Open Graph Explorer and sign-in to it.
overwrite the following URLs in any API and make sure you have selected 'GET'
To fetch all Custom Attributes -
https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/identity/userFlowAttributes?$filter=userFlowAttributeType
eq 'custom'
To fetch a specific attribute for an user, find the app-id of the application that is provided by MS for extensions (named as add-extensions-app) or simply make the call mentioned above to find the app-id and the attribute names.
https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/<upn
or object-id of user>?$select=mail,extension_<app-id-of-aad-extensions-app>_<attribute-name>
Make sure you grant the required permissions in 'Modify Permissions' tab

262374749.title: External Identity - Onboard external users via Self service
262374749.plain_text:
Dynamically Assigning users to Group
We can assign external users who sign up using self-service sign-in flow into a group by selecting 'Dynamic User' option in a group. We can write the query based on built-in attribute or extension(custom) attribute
Password Reset
We can let the user perform self service password reset (
SSPR
) by setting this. Check for 'password reset' in the main search bar and go to properties.
Enable self-service sign-up for your tenant
Before you can add a self-service sign-up user flow to your applications, you need to enable the feature for your tenant.
Go to '
Microsoft Entra id → External Identities → External Collaboration Settings
'
Create an application that will be linked with user flow in later steps
To link this user flow to application, register an app first '
Microsoft Entra id → App Registrations → New Registration' .
Git it a name and select Register
With in the just created application, select 'Quickstart' and the select the Angular as shown below
Select on 'Make these chagnes for me' in Step 1. This adds redirect URI
http://localhost:4200
into the app which we left blank while registering.
Click on 'Download' in step2 which downloads the sample application which has to be run on the system where 'portal.azure.com' is open.
To perform steps in Step 4, make sure you have Node js then Go to Downloads , uninstall the package and CD to location where you can find package.json
To start the project click on npm start
Now, come back here after you go to  'Create the user flow for self service sign-up'  section and perform all the steps.
You can open the application in browser '
http://localhost:4200'
and click on 'Login
You will now get a login screen. Click on 'Create One' as shown below
Since we have provided with 'Sign in with one time pass code' as identity provider it sends code to the personal emailid given
You are then take to a confirmation page
It then asks for attributes we have chosen
Move through by accepting. It shows what we can do and asks for acceptance
Now, we can see the login is successful. We can logout if we wish
We can now verify that the user is added and 'creation type' is shown as 'Self service'
Create the user flow for self-service sign-up
A self-service sign-up user flow creates a sign-up experience for the application you're providing to external users. You can configure user flow settings to control how the user signs up for the application:
Account types used for sign-in, such as social accounts like Facebook, or Microsoft Entra accounts
Attributes to be collected from the user signing up, such as first name, postal code, or country/region of residency
The user can sign in to your application, via the web, mobile, desktop, or single-page application (SPA). The application initiates an authorization request to the user flow-provided endpoint. The user flow defines and controls the user's experience. When the user completes the sign-up user flow, Microsoft Entra ID generates a token and redirects the user back to your application. Upon completion of sign-up, an account is provisioned for the user in the directory. Multiple applications can use the same user flow.
Go to '
Microsoft Entra id → External Identities → User Flow' and click on 'New User Flow
'
You can provide a name of the user flow which is prefixed with 'B2X_1_' (Labelled 1) ,  select the identity provider (Labelled 2) and select the attributes(Labelled 3) that are shown to customer while signing up
Once after the user flow is created, you can edit customer attributes, page layout, identity providers etc by selecting that user flow.
Go to 'Applications' from the menu with in the user flow page , add the application as shown

310973127.title: Networking Checklist
310973127.plain_text:
To Successfully access your application ensure these checks are done
WAF Firewall
If you have any resource like Load Balancer, CloudFront, API Gateway or similar that is using WAF, ensure that this CIDR(192.80.244.0/24) is whitelisted. For example, below are the steps to verify whitelisting of a cloud fronted application...
CIDR should not be added manually but added via TF while setting up S3 web.
Work is in progress to get a CIDR that is specific to Application Proxy Connectors which will be replace the current CIDR (192.80.244.0/24) that has wider range
To view ACL, go to CloudFront page in AWS UI and select the respective application related entry form the list (if the firewall associated with the application is known, we can switch to step 5 directly)
Switch to 'Security' tab in CloudFront entry to identify the firewall associated with it
Click on that firewall, select 'Rules' tab and click on the rule that has 'Allow' as action
Expected CIDR needs to be part of this list, if not application team needs to get the CIDR added to this list using Terraform Automation
Application hosted on Private Network
App Proxy needs to have visibility to the application. For example if the intended application that needs to be proxied has a destination url '
https://engage-queries-dev.nonprod.pge.com'
then this URL should be visible to App Proxy.
Public Hosted application:
If the application is hosted pubilcly(url can he accessed publicly) then no additional setup is required.
Private Hosted application:
If the application is hosted privately, we need to connect the VPC that the application is hosted on with the VPC app proxy is on. Following are details of App Proxy VPC
Region:
West 2
VPC
:
vpc-096aba9a97a08eb5d
AWS Account
: 381492164201
Custom domain - DNS
If the application URL (ex workviz-dev.msappproxy.net) has a domain msappproxy.net which is a default one, no DNS check is required in that case

TF Code

TF code
for achieving the same
Example

514712703977- PGE-LZCommonVPC-Dev) for Hosted Zone Name(
nonprod.pge.com). Below screenshot shows mapping of engage-dev.nonprod.pge.com to engage-dev-pge.msappproxy.net

229312935.title: Local Setup: How to Add and Expose a Static WebSite on AWS EC2 for Remote Access through App Proxy in Microsoft Entra ID
229312935.plain_text:
Pre-requisites:
Get yourself an Entra ID Administrator identity, ie tahvadmin@lab.pge.com, you will fetch your password every 5 days from
Connectivity to/from Azure AD Domain, lab.pge.com, Domain controllers (10.94.0.36 & 10.94.0.37) from AWS Subnet(s)
nslookup 10.94.0.36; nslookup lab.pge.com
Deploy an EC2 with PGE's Golden Windows AMI using terraform.  See
https://github.com/pgetech/tf-module-test/tree/s7aw/ec2/pge_windows
Join that EC2 Windows Server with Entra Domain Services managed domain, lab.pge.com. See
https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/active-directory-domain-services/join-windows-vm
Find or Create your Entra ID Application Proxy Connector Group.  See the Azure/Home/Application proxy console.
Configure an Must we do any configuration (PAPM) to permit RDP?
Part One(1) Installing and Registering the App Proxy Connector:
Sign onto PAPM to fetch/create your temporary Entra ID adminstrator's password.
Browse to the AWS EC2 Console and copy the Private IP address of the target EC2.
Install an RDP Client onto your workstation and Add PC; populate PC name: with IP address; push Add
Double-click the target PC connection and enter your admin credentials.
Launch IE and Install Microsoft Edge.
Add a tab and open the edge settings then set Edge as your default  Browser,
Using Edge, navigate to
https://portal.azure.com
and sign in using your administrator lanid @ domain.
Push Continue
Sign on with administrator's lanid only.
From the Home page, choose "App proxy",
Choose "Download connector service"
Push "Accept terms & Download", open the downloaded file
Open File Explorer, navigate to your Downloads folder, run the AADApplicationProxyConnectorInstaller.exe as administrator.
Pushing yes...
Push install to complete the installation wizard...
Enter your Administrator credentials to complete the registration process.
Now you should see the target host's connector added to your connector group.
And flip-flop, confirm that your target EC2's App Proxy is healthy.
Open the Windows Services Manager, services.msc.  Check to see if the status for the following two services is
Running
.
Microsoft Entra application proxy Connector
enables connectivity.
Microsoft Entra application proxy Connector Updater
is an automated update service.
If they are NOT running, right-click and try to start them. You are DONE installing and registering App Proxy Connector.
Part Two(2) Installing the windows web server, IIS:
Given PGE's Golden AMI is based on Windows Server 2019, Internet Information Service is NOT installed by default.  So, we have to install it.
Command-line installation?
Or Via the console?  Start Windows Server Manager; navigate to Manage/Add Roles and Features.
Choose Installation Type/Role-based...; push Next.
Your local, target server should be selected by default; press Next.
Check "Web Server (IIS)"
Push Add Feature.
Push Next(s)... accept the default "Role Services", next; Push Install.
Push Close.
This is what DONE looks like; confirm that your IIS is a healthy GREEN.
Optional, Part Three(3) Configuring a custom web-site:
Command line? in line 2, replace the types with parameter values.
Or console? Start Windows Server Manager; navigate to Manage/Add Roles and Features.
In the
Connections
pane, right-click the
Sites
node in the tree, and then click
Add Website
.
Fill out the form; I entered a name, path, port 8000 and accepted the defaults.
Need a homepage?  try this...
https://www.tutorialstonight.com/sample-html-code-for-homepage#google_vignette
In
Features View
of IIS Manager, double-click
Authentication
.
Choose to Edit "Anonymous Authentication"; select "Application pool identity"; push OK.
Confirm IIS sees your homepage, my file is named index.html.  In
Features View
of IIS Manager, double-click
Default Document
.
Test it with a browser.  Enter
http://locahost
to see the default windows site.
Part Four(4) Configuring DNS, HTTPS/TLS:
To be SAF2 compliant(encryption of data in-transit),  we configure IIS with HTTPS/TLS Certificate.
Contact Active Directory team and request your internal domain name, ie
pgec2site.lab.pge.com
, and IP address be added to AADDS.
Use this form,
SampleINF.txt
to Request a TLS/HTTPS certificate for your endpoint.
How to submit-retrieve a CSR - Windows computer - command line.txt
My example request text file, pgec2site.txt, looks like this...
[Version]
Signature="$Windows NT$"
[NewRequest]
Subject = "CN=
pgec2site.lab.pge.com
,OU=Development,O=Pacific Gas and Electric Company,L=San Francisco,ST=California,C=US" ; Remove to use an empty Subject name.
;Because SSL/TLS does not require a Subject name when a SAN extension is included, the certificate Subject name can be empty.
;If you are using another protocol, verify the certificate requirements.
;EncipherOnly = FALSE ; Only for Windows Server 2003 and Windows XP. Remove for all other client operating system versions.
Exportable = TRUE   ; TRUE = Private key is exportable
KeyLength = 2048     ; Valid key sizes: 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384
KeySpec = 1          ; Key Exchange ? Required for encryption
KeyUsage = 0xA0      ; Digital Signature, Key Encipherment
MachineKeySet = True
ProviderName = "Microsoft RSA SChannel Cryptographic Provider"
RequestType = PKCS10 ; or CMC.
[EnhancedKeyUsageExtension]
; If you are using an enterprise CA the EnhancedKeyUsageExtension section can be omitted
OID=1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.1 ; Server Authentication
OID=1.3.6.1.5.5.7.3.2 ; Client Authentication
[Extensions]
; If your client operating system is Windows Server 2008, Windows Server 2008 R2, Windows Vista, or Windows 7
; SANs can be included in the Extensions section by using the following text format. Note 2.5.29.17 is the OID for a SAN extension.
2.5.29.17 = "{text}"
_continue_ = "dns=
pgec2site.lab.pge.com
&"
_continue_ = "email=tahv@
pge.com
&"
_continue_ = "email=CYBERSECURITYServicesCloudSecurityEmployees@
pge.com
&"
; Notes:
; a. remove DNS entries that are not needed or add entries if more names are needed
; b. the common name is required and should be the first dns= entry
; c. supply at least two PG&E email contacts, an Exchange distribution list is a good option.
; d. update the OU= entry above with: Development, QA, Production, Sandbox, Test
I open a terminal as an Admin in my EC2; issue this command to generate the certificate request file.
certreq -new pgec2site.txt pgec2site.req which looks like this.
-----BEGIN NEW CERTIFICATE REQUEST----- MIIFETCCA/kCAQAwgZsxCzAJBgNVBAYTAlVTMRMwEQYDVQQIDApDYWxpZm9ybmlh MRYwFAYDVQQHDA1TYW4gRnJhbmNpc2NvMSkwJwYDVQQKDCBQYWNpZmljIEdhcyBh bmQgRWxlY3RyaWMgQ29tcGFueTEUMBIGA1UECwwLRGV2ZWxvcG1lbnQxHjAcBgNV BAMMFXBnZWMyc2l0ZS5sYWIucGdlLmNvbTCCASIwDQYJKoZIhvcNAQEBBQADggEP ADCCAQoCggEBAOw2sliCLFIOB3ddpELAStsJeLjx8jwPTlcKnyFA+qPW79Kp1tMQ 8EYaA6Rc7iZIdTIQmSPxogKb5sTS289vFQAL5yTZGdKGCoVuNjR+4iQB4mCDrJFw j+ROZv+0DUYMICM6oAoVR4VF6N9Q+3GXnDOinCk7KzTqLP7FcQDV15ofUVUPmUTG mDz7sU5kk+VxLN4lPwqu9Oo9mpeSu4VtJj+w9fanyoDshf8D+AGNWUq5RdGWb8KP 1ihelnHjJBJ2bv8pux8cbMq/d4O1+2PB96yeblvsTa23UcyRxsojRDFoEoUvOv3s 7HKCOqB/sc5qF0g/3k60CAYxt0IBAyEsBX0CAwEAAaCCAi4wHAYKKwYBBAGCNw0C AzEOFgwxMC4wLjE3NzYzLjIwVgYJKwYBBAGCNxUUMUkwRwIBCQwbRUMyQU1BWi1G Q01HRlBHLmxhYi5wZ2UuY29tDBhFQzJBTUFaLUZDTUdGUEdcc3NtLXVzZXIMC2Nl cnRyZXEuZXhlMHIGCisGAQQBgjcNAgIxZDBiAgEBHloATQBpAGMAcgBvAHMAbwBm AHQAIABSAFMAQQAgAFMAQwBoAGEAbgBuAGUAbAAgAEMAcgB5AHAAdABvAGcAcgBh AHAAaABpAGMAIABQAHIAbwB2AGkAZABlAHIDAQAwggFABgkqhkiG9w0BCQ4xggEx MIIBLTAOBgNVHQ8BAf8EBAMCBaAwHQYDVR0lBBYwFAYIKwYBBQUHAwEGCCsGAQUF BwMCMGMGA1UdEQRcMFqCFXBnZWMyc2l0ZS5sYWIucGdlLmNvbYEMdGFodkBwZ2Uu Y29tgTNDWUJFUlNFQ1VSSVRZU2VydmljZXNDbG91ZFNlY3VyaXR5RW1wbG95ZWVz QHBnZS5jb20weAYJKoZIhvcNAQkPBGswaTAOBggqhkiG9w0DAgICAIAwDgYIKoZI hvcNAwQCAgCAMAsGCWCGSAFlAwQBKjALBglghkgBZQMEAS0wCwYJYIZIAWUDBAEC MAsGCWCGSAFlAwQBBTAHBgUrDgMCBzAKBggqhkiG9w0DBzAdBgNVHQ4EFgQUPTbl 1G6tY+fbjhm0nwpczyC34/QwDQYJKoZIhvcNAQEFBQADggEBAL+UaC7DjbGMmR87 0s7nxoxQq9otCyUC8sWDLwisPkLnnyvx6qDdZCwZeIfHZKWzw03+dQsdKzjmU6W1 5UBJjPbzzomAD5mAhE3Rx4jJEHLr+YqobyqXbT0IToT/Dh9B9040OHYXBElYcsa8 VE57sM84t+B0j7DojDr0Im8O9YuuZD9p5svPJV5Bm5rtH3o2zLNH8P3ZFom9o7pq CC9yTwtTCclMw2QSvlRrRBmX/aLUnvQOsAPmTHZMga7t/oxCkxUDsJ+QT2iHxYOx HgC13wVGxy/B5id8sCefuS/qCrmfU7wEAbiFzPVDb2AekpiL2NI4xVBQtqYFh5Pf hocSy9I=-----END NEW CERTIFICATE REQUEST-----
Given my EC2 has no connectivity to the on-premise certificate request endpoint, "
rcdesecapxx0007.lab.pge.com
\PGE Test Issuing CA 1", I navigate my desktop browser to
https://pgetestca.pge.com/certsrv/certrqxt.asp
and manually enter my request.
Wait a couple of days and check to see if your pending certificate request has been issued. Visit
https://pgetestca.pge.com/certsrv/certckpn.asp
Push the Download CA certificate and figure out a way to copy it to the IIS host.
Options include S3 bucket; copy/paste file to/from clipboard with RDP; share your workstation folder via RDP.
Install the TLS Certificate into IIS.  Open IIS Manager, navigate to Server Certificates.
Choose Complete Certificate Request
Specify the certificate file you downloaded in step 6. Use the Personal certificate store.
Enable the installed certificate by adding an HTTPS binding to your website. Navigate to website/bindings
Part Five(5) Exposing the web-site:
Now that we've prepared our environment and installed a connector, we're ready to add on-premises applications to Microsoft Entra ID.
Sign in to the
Microsoft Entra admin center
as at least a
Application Administrator
.
Browse to
Identity
>
Applications
>
Enterprise applications
.
Select
New application/Create your own application
.
Select Add your own on-premise app, specify the app-name and press
Next
.
Populate the form, Name it with your domain Internal Url is https your FQDN, External Url is automatically populated, Pre Autheintication Passthrough, Connector Group that the host is a member of
Once the Enterprise Application, aka AWS Application, is configured then navigate to
Identity
>
Applications
>
Enterprise applications
.
Search for your application by name and edit these properties...
Uncheck
Validate Backend SSL Certificate
Select Passthrough for Pre Authentication

168199260.title: STRIDE Model
168199260.plain_text:
Threat-1
Threat attributes
Use Case Name
Authenticate, Spoof User Identity, Spoof AWS SFTP Service Endpoint, Spoof Notification(s), Spoof Email Sender, Spoof GeoLocation, CRUD User
Threat agent or source
External threats
includes but are not limited to hackers, cybercriminal organizations and state-sponsored actors.
Goals
Reliable Identity
Trustworthy Authentication
Accurate Access Management
Exploit details
A spoofing attack is a situation in which a person or program successfully identifies as another by falsifying data, to gain an illegitimate advantage.
Threat Category (STRIDE)
SPOOFING
Potential threat countermeasures or architecture approaches as threat responses
SFTP encrypts authentication
being transferred over the SSH data stream.
Require clients store private-key(s) in a secure vault
.  This option may protect the theft of private-key(s) should the 3rd party system be compromised.
Use IP blacklisting and whitelisting.
It may be necessary to deploy SFTP Server in private VPC to block access then manage a white-list of our approved 3rd parties ip addresses. This can be implemented with AWS Web Application Firewall (WAF) and/or VPC hosted endpoints and VPC SecurityGroups.
Issue certificates to external users, 3rd parties.
This option will ensure that anyone accessing your system has a PGE-security certificate to verify who they are. This can be implemented with MTLS protocol and AWS Certificate Manager service.
SFTP doesn’t stop the unauthorized transfer of data to third parties.
This can lead to non-compliant disclosures of data, which may break regulatory rules on confidentiality and privacy.
Threat-2
Threat attributes
Use Case Name
Tamper with Data-In-Transit, Tamper with File(s)Data-at-Rest, Tamper with Credentials?, Tamper with Data-In-Use,  Tamper with Notifications, Tamper with Logs, Encrypt by DataClassification?  Crack Encryption(s) AES, KMS, TLS?
Threat agent or source
Those working inside an organization
may be a significant cause of elevated privileges.
External threats
includes but are not limited to hackers, cybercriminal organizations and state-sponsored actors.
Goals
Data Integrity
Notification Integrity
Protect by Data Classification = Restricted, Confidential, Internal, Public
Exploit details
Those working inside an organization
may be a significant cause of tampering.
External threats
includes but are not limited to hackers, cybercriminal organizations and state-sponsored actors.
Threat Category (STRIDE)
TAMPERING
Potential threat countermeasures or architecture approaches
Unique Customer Managed Key(s) per Workflow Instance;  Managed by AWS KMS; used by AWS S3.
SFTP encrypts everything being transferred over the SSH data stream; from the authentication of the users to the actual files being transferred, if any part of the data is intercepted, it will be unreadable because of the encryption.
Configure SSH with AES-256 cipher-algorithms
SSH uses a hashing algorithm, usually SHA-2, to determine data integrity.
AWS Hardened SFTP Service
Use Digital Signatures (MD5) for Files & Notifications.
Threat-3
Threat attributes
Use Case Name
Report on Authentication(s), Report on Workflow Transaction(s), Report by User-Identity, Transaction-Identity, Workflow-Instance, Sign File-Transfer, Sign Notification
Threat agent or source
Both legitimate users and criminal perpetrators.
Goals
The inability to deny responsibility for performing a specific act.
Assurance that the sender of information is provided with proof of delivery and the recipient is provided with proof of the sender’s identity, so neither can later deny having processed the information.
Protection against an individual falsely denying having performed a particular action. Provides the capability to determine whether a given individual took a particular action such as creating information, sending a message, approving information, and receiving a message.
Exploit details
Nonrepudiation is achieved through cryptography, like digital signatures,
and includes other services for authentication, auditing and logging. In online transactions, digital signatures ensure that a party cannot later deny sending information or deny the authenticity of its signature.
SFTP does not centralize audits , logs or documentation.
Most compliance frameworks require some documentation to demonstrate compliance. SFTP can include audit logs, but without a centralized SFTP server documenting access across multiple systems can make documentation hard and raise red flags for assessors. Likewise, documentation must also adhere to privacy laws, which becomes exponentially more difficult over multiple SFTP servers.
Include documentation and auditing.
Document things like compliance and file access. Monitor file access as well as document things like user consent and other requests.
Threat Category (STRIDE)
REPUDIATION
Potential threat countermeasures or architecture approaches
Use digital signature(s) to support a determination of whether a message was actually signed by a given entity.
To mitigate the risk of people repudiating their own signatures, the standard approach is to involve a trusted third party.
Use CCOE's Centralized Logging: AWS CloudTrail & CloudWatch Logs
Each Workflow Instance has an identity; searchable in CloudTrail logs.
Each Transaction has an identity; searchable/traceable in CloudTrail logs.
Each File has identity; searchable/traceable in CloudTrail logs.
CloudTrail logs searchable by User identity.
Threat-4
Threat attributes
Use Case Name
Assign File PGE DataClassification, Configure Users, Folders and Roles per Workflow Instance
Threat agent or source
Those working inside an organization
are a significant cause of data breaches.
External threats
includes but are not limited to hackers, cybercriminal organizations and state-sponsored actors.
Goals
Trust & Privacy of Data, ie Personally Identifiable Information
Data Loss Prevention
Enforcement of Data Classification Policies
Reasonable Storage Costs
Exploit details
This may also be known as
data privacy
or
data breach
.  Data privacy is challenging since it attempts to use data while protecting an individual's privacy preferences and personally identifiable information.  A data breach is a security violation, in which sensitive, protected or confidential data is copied, transmitted, viewed, stolen or used by an individual unauthorized to do so.
Threat Category (STRIDE)
INFORMATION DISCLOSURE
Potential threat countermeasures or architecture approaches
Use folder security for internal access.
Access controls can be a pain to set up because somebody has to do it manually on individual folders. Business users typically don’t have the skills or permission to do this, so organizations often resort to these users writing help desk tickets for IT to undertake access management tasks.
Implement trust boundaries,
aka multi-tenant isolation, using AWS S3 Bucket Policy + S3-Labels(aka Folders) + KMS Key Policy.
Practice the principle of least privilege
when configuring AWS DataTransferUser + ssh-key + IAM RBAC.
Require clients store private-key(s) and signatures in a secure vault
.  This option may protect the theft of private-key(s) should the 3rd party system be compromised.
Use CCOEs
to label artifacts to facilitate identification, protection and notification.
Use PGE
to identify sensitivity of data.
Business defined data storage policies; use S3 Storage Class Analysis.
Threat-5
Threat attributes
Use Case Name
Connect, Authenticate, Send, Receive
Threat agent or source
Criminal perpetrators of DoS attacks often target sites or services hosted on high-profile web servers such as banks or credit card payment gateways. Revenge, blackmail and hacktivism can motivate these attacks.
Goals
Prevent the
crash or flooding of services.
Minimize application downtime and latency.
Scrub bad traffic at specific layers.
Exploit details
A
denial-of-service attack (DoS attack)
is a cyber-attack in which the perpetrator seeks to make a machine or network resource unavailable to its intended users by temporarily or indefinitely disrupting services of a host connected to a network. Denial of service is typically accomplished by flooding the targeted machine or resource with superfluous requests in an attempt to overload systems and prevent some or all legitimate requests from being fulfilled. In a
distributed denial-of-service attack (DDoS attack)
, the incoming traffic flooding the victim originates from many different sources.
Threat Category (STRIDE)
DENIAL OF SERVICE
Potential threat countermeasures or architecture approaches
Protect the AWS Application Load Balancers and SFTP servers behind a firewall
, use AWS Web Application Firewall (WAF) with ELBs in Transit account; target auto-scaling-groups within the private VPC/subnets of partner accounts.
Protect service endpoints with security group(s) attached to VPCs, subnets.
Maximize application availability and responsiveness with managed DDoS protection.  Configure AWS Shield.
Use AWS Shield to configure static thresholds to protect services.
To integrate logging with PGE's SIOC so they
can detect and mitigate attacks,  use CCOE's Centralized Logging infrastructure.
Threat-6
Threat attributes
Use Case Name
CRUD Workflow(s), CRUD User(s), Spoof ssh-Session, Guess Adminstrator Password
Threat agent or source
Those working inside an organization
are a significant cause of elevated privileges.
External threats
includes but are not limited to hackers, cybercriminal organizations and state-sponsored actors.
Goals
Improve administrative efficiency with a self-service kiosk for CRUDing users.
User-Privilege oversight and governance.
Prevent Horizontal privilege escalation between tenants.
Prevent Vertical privilege escalation.
Exploit details
Privilege escalation
is the act of exploiting a
bug
, a design flaw, or a configuration oversight in an
operating system
or
software application
to gain elevated access to
resources
that are normally protected from an application or
user
. The result is that an application with more
privileges
than intended.
Vertical privilege escalation
, also known as
privilege elevation
, where a lower privilege user or application accesses functions or content reserved for higher privilege users or applications.
Horizontal privilege escalation
, where a normal user accesses functions or content reserved for other normal users.
Threat Category (STRIDE)
ELEVATION OF PRIVILEGE
Potential threat countermeasures or architecture approaches
Configure S3, KMS & IAM to isolate / encapsulate Workflow Instances(aka Tenants)
.
Configure IAM-Policies to separate Administrator and User-Roles
.
Require clients store private-key(s) and signatures in a secure vault
.  This option may protect the theft of private-key(s) should the 3rd party system be compromised.
Implement a self-service kiosk for Administrative tasks; use PGE LanID, SSO, MEA workflow to authorize kiosk access.
Practice the principle of least privilege
when configuring AWS DataTransferUser + ssh-key + IAM RBAC.

50856963.title: Deploy SPA for PingOne (Implicit) Demo
50856963.plain_text:
Implicit Flow
The Open ID Connect Implicit flow is recommended for native apps and websites that have no dynamic web server (such as a single-page-application deployed via S3/Cloudfront). In such scenarios, everything that is passed between the app or site and the IdP can be viewed using browser development tools.
In the Implicit flow, a public/private key (JSON Web Key or JWK) scheme is used to encrypt or sign user details. When you register your client app with the IdP (PingOne), you will receive a client ID and you will register valid redirect uri's with the PingOne authorization server. In an Implicit flow, registration of the redirect_uri is necessary to ensure the users will not be tricked into visiting a malicious client site. The authorization server will never redirect the user to an invalid redirect_uri.
In Step 1, the user attempts to start a session with your client app and is redirected to the OpenID Provider (PingOne), passing in the client ID, which is unique for that application.
In Step 2, the OpenID Provider authenticates and authorizes the user for a particular application instance.
In Step 3, user details are encoded by the OpenID Provider into an id_token (JWT) that contains user information (scopes and claims, in OAuth/OIDC terms) and signature (using RS256), which is passed to a preconfigured redirect_uri that exists in the application (callback)
In Step 4, the client app confirms the JWT id_token and confirms the signature using the public key. If everything is fine, a session is established for the user.
In the Implicit flow, the transaction is secure despite the fact that the client app cannot be authenticated as it exists only in the browser or mobile device. Authentication can be trusted because the IdP sends tokens encrypted using a public/private key scheme and will only send tokens to the preconfigured Redirect URI. In other words, someone could steal the public key and client id, but that doesn’t matter, because only the IdP has the proper information (the redirect URI for the intended client app and the private key) to use the public key and client ID correctly.
PingOne configuration
Create a OIDC client called React-App1-Implicit
Use Grant type:
Implicit
In the Authentication Flow tab, provide the following URL values
Start SSO URL:
https://d2z8b22s4ylwto.cloudfront.net/login
Redirect (Callback) URI:
https://d2z8b22s4ylwto.cloudfront.net/authentication/callback
Add the following attributes to be returned by the User Info endpoint
Choose the below allowed Scopes for this client
Map the attributes for the User Info endpoint
Deployment instructions
Source code can be found at
https://github.com/sunshiva-pge/pingone-oidc-spa.git
Demo URLs

51055208.title: PingFederate Improvements for L&M
51055208.plain_text:
Summary
Several changes are planned in an effort to improve the availability of PG&E's PingFederate SSO service.  Although the existing architecture satisfies the requirements for PG&E Tier 1 applications (<4 Hour RTO), there have been several incidents in the past year which have impacted the ability for users to authenticate using PingFederate.
Our proposal for changes in the near-term (Q1 2019) include the following:
Simplify the traffic path to the PingFederate servers, removing components which have historically caused downtime
Expand monitoring to identify a wider range of potential problems
Improve observability by integrating with Splunk and AppDynamics
In the long term, we are planning to move infrastructure to AWS.  Additional monitoring will provide us with service availability metrics so that we can measure our progress.
Simplify the traffic path
Prior to February 2019, a complex DNS and load balancing configuration was in place in order to support MobileIron Device Posture checking.  This component has been moved to a separate load balancer VIP.
Expand monitoring
We will implement a synthetic transaction health check.  This check will simulate a user logging in every minute, both from the Internet and from within the PG&E network, and against each of the 8 PingFederate servers.  This health check will help quickly detect when one server appears healthy, but is experiencing an issue.
The health check will be run in AWS and monitored with CloudWatch sending alerts directly to ENOC.  CloudWatch will provide historical service availability metrics.
Improve observability
By the end of February, logs will be sent to Splunk and the PingFederate App and Dashboards will be installed.  Approved users will be able to view detailed metrics and logs for PingFederate.  For example, number of successful and failed logins with error codes.
AppDynamics (pending approval) will provide even more visibility into the health of PingFederate, including mapping network connections between PingFederate and its upstream and downstream dependencies.
Architecture Diagram

50856965.title: Deploy Webapp for PingOne (Auth Code) Demo
50856965.plain_text:
Authorization Code Flow
The Authorization Code flow is an option for apps that have web-server logic that enables back-end communication with the IdP (PingOne). It functions like a traditional three-legged OAuth2 and results in an OAuth access token being returned to the web application via hidden, encrypted calls made entirely on the back end. In this flow, the end-user authorizes the client, and the IdP sends a special, one-time-use code that can be exchanged by the back-end server for an OAuth access token. This exchange needs to include the client id and client secret in addition to the code, just like a traditional OAuth 2.0 Authorization Code Grant. It is more secure than the Implicit flow, because tokens are not visible through the browser and the client app can also be authenticated.
In Step 1, the user attempts to start a session with your client app and is redirected to the OpenID Provider (OneLogin), passing in the client ID, which is unique for that application.
In Step 2, the OpenID Provider authenticates the user, and the user authorizes permissions for the client application to access the user-authorized resources.
In Step 3, a one-time-use code is passed back to the web server using a registered redirect_uri.
In Step 4, the web server passes the code, client ID, and client secret to the OpenID Provider’s token endpoint, and the OpenID Provider validates the code and returns a one-hour access token.
In Step 5, the web server uses the access token to get further details about the user (if necessary) and establishes a session for the user.
PingOne configuration
Create a OIDC client called Nodejs-App1-Code
Use Grant type:
Authorization Code

https://pingone-oidc-dev.7ypz2h2yyg.us-west-1.elasticbeanstalk.com/auth
Redirect (Callback) URI:
https://pingone-oidc-dev.7ypz2h2yyg.us-west-1.elasticbeanstalk.com/auth/callback
Add the following attributes to be returned by the User Info endpoint
Choose the below allowed Scopes for this client
